Atmos7.3
Dome Y2K Zero
Classic progressive psytrance with steady grooves and a clean, hypnotic flow. Atmos is often a strong match for the author's taste, although a previous Boom performance felt less convincing than expected.

11:30
Arökem4.5-7.5
Cooking Groove
Arökem seems to move between slow, chilled material and fuller live arrangements with guitar, flute, and electronic elements. The instrumental passages connected well with the author, while the vocals were less appealing, making this an interesting option rather than a must-see.

Dreamweaving Yoga Nidra

With Maddie Jones.

Pumpui
13:00
Frederika6.5-8.0
Cooking Groove
Multicultural downtempo and psybient with Arabic and African touches, tribal rhythms, and some EDM energy. Frederika could be a rewarding discovery for listeners who enjoy warm global sounds, especially if there is no stronger clash in the timetable.

Sebastian Venu6.2-7.8
Cooking Groove
Psychedelic live electronica with flute, electronic drums, forest-like textures, and touches of Latin and techno energy. Sebastian Venu could appeal to listeners who enjoy organic instrumentation inside a varied electronic set.

20:00
O:P:E:N:I:N:G
Ozora Stage
20:30
Hilight Tribe6.0-8.1; 7.0 overall
Ozora Stage
High-energy tribal trance built around live percussion and an organic festival atmosphere. Hilight Tribe feels like a lively way to begin the festival for the author, even if not every track lands equally well.
21:00
Interactive Exhibition & Live Painting
Mirador
Visionary art exhibition with interactive works by Davide Gomba / Lutopia, Neuromorph, Adél Garics & Péter Fejes, Skywalker Visual, Deepversive Studio, Balázs Fügedi & Manó Tasi, Emma Forgách and Zsanett Kalácska; plus live painters.
21:00
O:P:E:N:I:N:G
The Dome
21:30
Blacksmith Show
Ironia
21:30
Solar Fields6.5-7.5
The Dome
Atmospheric ambient and psybient with expansive textures, patient progression, and a cinematic emotional quality. Solar Fields could appeal to listeners looking for an immersive Dome journey rather than a conventional dancefloor set.
22:00
Brooklyn Gypsies4-7.5
Dragon Nest
Live global fusion with Arabic influences, Spanish-language vocals, and a strong band feeling. Brooklyn Gypsies could appeal to listeners who want a multicultural break from electronic-only sets.
22:00
Humanoids5.0-6.5
Ozora Stage
Fast, spacey full-on psytrance around 145–148 BPM, with playful but darker and more twisted elements. Humanoids may suit listeners who enjoy relentless intergalactic energy, although this style is no longer a close match for the author's personal taste.
22:00
Gaudi
Pumpui
23:00
Stargazing
Microcosmos
23:30
Fire Show
Dragon Nest
23:30
Koxbox4.0-6.0
Ozora Stage
Old-school psychedelic trance with eccentric sound design and a distinctly classic character. Koxbox is not a close match for the author's taste, but the music may appeal to listeners who enjoy the roots and stranger edges of psytrance.
23:30
Entheogenic8.5
The Dome
Rich psybient and psychedelic downtempo with organic detail, deep atmosphere, and an immersive musical flow. Entheogenic is one of the author's strongest recommendations for listeners who enjoy emotional, layered journeys away from the main dancefloor.

Tuesday 28 July

00:00
Heavy Hertz
Pumpui
00:30
Óperentzia4.2-8.2
Dragon Nest
A broad live fusion that can move between psytrance and full-band arrangements with flute, keyboards, drums, and other instruments. Operentzia varies widely in how well it matches the author's taste, but the best instrumental passages could make the set well worth exploring.
01:00
Ajja
Ozora Stage
01:30
Regan
Pumpui
01:30
Vibrasphere6.3-7.8
The Dome
Melodic progressive psytrance with warm grooves, emotional movement, and a spacious, immersive flow. Vibrasphere could appeal to listeners who enjoy patient journeys that develop without becoming overly aggressive.
02:30
Populous6.3-7.5
Dragon Nest
Relaxed instrumental electronica with minimal foundations and touches of live-sounding musical texture. Populous is easy to listen to and could work well for a gentle reset, although the author does not consider it essential.
02:30
Fantazma
Ozora Stage
03:00
Tongue & Groove
Pumpui
03:00
Yoy Project8.6
The Dome
Dark, beautifully detailed downtempo with a deep and immersive atmosphere. Yoy Project is among the strongest downtempo discoveries in this guide, though the 03:00 set time may make it difficult for the author to catch.

11:30
Spinal Fusion5.8-6.7
Ozora Stage
Daytime progressive and full-on psytrance with a familiar, accessible dancefloor drive. Spinal Fusion does not feel as distinctive to the author as Ritmo or Headroom, but the set could still be enjoyable in the right company and festival mood.
12:00
Your Wild & wise in motion
Bodhi Tent · Eva Deleau
With Eva Deleau.
12:00
Visions of Water
Chambok House · Mercuri Amaryllis
Led by Mercuri Amaryllis.
12:00
Earthly Measures4.5-8.2
Cooking Groove
Eclectic global electronica with warm organic grooves, downtempo movement, and sounds drawn from different musical cultures. Earthly Measures could appeal to listeners who enjoy adventurous but approachable journeys beyond conventional psytrance.
12:00
At Home in your Body: Somatic Workshop

12:00
Eat Static4.0-8.0
The Dome
Psychedelic electronic music with a darker, energetic edge and a wide stylistic range. Eat Static may be especially interesting for listeners who appreciate unusual crossover territory, though its heavier moments do not always match the author's taste.

Starlab6.0-7.3
Ozora Stage
Modern psytrance with a strong progressive and full-on drive. StarLab's nighttime sets can feel slightly too hard for the author, but the daytime slot is especially promising and likely to be a better match.

Bellegance & Fraser6.7-7.7
Cooking Groove
Progressive electronic music with a steady, flowing dancefloor character. Bellegance & Fraser could appeal to listeners who enjoy gradual development and a less aggressive festival groove.

Kalki7.5
Ozora Stage
Melodic progressive psytrance with polished production and an uplifting, accessible flow. Kalki is a new discovery for the author and feels worth experiencing live for the first time.

15:30
Ko Shin Moon4.0-6.5
Cooking Groove
Live psychedelic fusion with guitar, Eastern influences, and prominent vocals. Ko Shin Moon is not consistently aligned with the author's taste, but the instrumental and less vocal passages could be rewarding for listeners drawn to organic cross-cultural sounds.
16:00
Ace Ventura8.3
Ozora Stage
A major progressive psytrance act known for powerful grooves, polished builds, and a highly effective main-stage flow. Ace Ventura is a strong choice for listeners who want a classic, dependable psytrance experience.

Nana Benz Du Togo4.6
Dragon Nest
Energetic African live music driven by rhythm, voices, and an organic band presence. Nana Benz Du Togo is not a close match for the author's electronic preferences, but could be an excellent choice for listeners seeking a lively global-music experience.

Aux256.5
The Dome
Relaxed electronic music with a calm, restorative atmosphere. AUX25 could work well as a place to slow down, rest, and enjoy a softer part of the festival.

Out Of Orbit
Pumpui
19:30
Chicha Libre6.1-7.1
Dragon Nest
Latin American live music with guitar and a warm, rhythmic band feeling. Chica Libre could appeal to listeners who want an organic, danceable change of pace from the electronic stages.
19:30
Coloboma & Filip Varial4.7-7.3
The Dome
Coloboma & Filip Varial appears to connect Coloboma's psy-techno direction with Filip Varial's more psybient and atmospheric side. The latter resonated more strongly with the author, although the impression is based on very limited available material.
20:30
Circus Cabaret
Ambyss
20:30
Circus Cabaret
Cosmic Theater
20:30
Strontium Dogs4.5-7.8
Ozora Stage
Spacey psychedelic trance that can shift from bizarre, slightly disorienting textures into progressive and very danceable passages. Strontium Dogs may be worth exploring for adventurous listeners, although the author does not consider the set essential.

Zakhorov5.5-7.0
The Dome
Ethnotek and rhythm-focused electronic music with strong drum elements and possible movement into other styles. Zakhorov could be interesting to try, though the material heard so far did not fully connect with the author.
21:30
Blacksmith Show
Ironia
22:00
BCUC4.3
Dragon Nest
Intense African live music powered by percussion, repetition, and communal energy. BCUC is not tailored to the author's personal taste, but could strongly appeal to listeners who enjoy raw, physical band performances.
22:00
Para Halu
Ozora Stage
22:30
Sounds from the Melting Pot
Melting Pot
22:30
Prometheus
Pumpui
22:30
DJ Josko4.5-7.3
The Dome
Only limited material was available for DJ Josko, so the musical direction remains difficult to judge with confidence. This is best approached as a discovery set for curious listeners rather than a firm recommendation.

Smag På Dig Selv4.0/6.4

Wild live music with prominent saxophone and a restless, high-energy character. Smag Pa Dig Selv could appeal to listeners who enjoy unpredictable band performances and a more chaotic kind of movement.

Fidju Kitxora6.1-8.2
Dragon Nest
Lusophone live music with psychill atmosphere, organic performance, and moments that feel at home in the Dome. Fidju Kitxora could be well worth trying for listeners who are still awake and looking for something melodic, unusual, and culturally rich.

Sibu Manaï7-8.6
Cooking Groove
A multilingual live performance led by a striking voice and influences that move through Brazilian, reggae, and French musical territory. Sibu Manai could be a rewarding choice for listeners who value vocals, stylistic variety, and organic stage presence.

Perfect Sphere
Ozora Stage
Only a small amount of material could be confidently connected to Perfect Sphere, and it did not clearly suggest a typical psytrance direction. Treat this as an open discovery slot rather than a genre-specific recommendation.

Panache & Grabuge7.5
Cooking Groove
Electro-fusion with live flute, EDM energy, and forest-like psychedelic touches. Panache & Grabuge could be a fun discovery if the timetable allows, although the author found only a small amount of released material.

Freedom Fighters8.4-9.2; 8.6 overall
Ozora Stage
Deep, driving psytrance with precise production, hypnotic tension, and a strong progressive flow. Freedom Fighters is one of the author's highest recommendations, with the hope that this appearance focuses on psytrance rather than the artist's newer techno direction.

Giolibrí
Cooking Groove
Reliable information about Giolibri was limited when this guide was prepared. Treat the set as an open discovery opportunity rather than a recommendation based on a confident genre description.
16:00
Merkaba8
Ozora Stage
Slow to midtempo psybient and deep psychedelic bass with organic, tribal, and ceremonial textures. Merkaba feels more immersive and meditative than dancefloor-driven, making this a distinctive high-priority experience for listeners who enjoy richly textured journeys.

Made in Bass
Melting Pot
17:30
Marysia Osu & Kibir La Amlak6.0-8.0
Dragon Nest
Marysia Osu and Kibir La Amlak may bring together Osu's gentle, chilled musicality and La Amlak's reggae-rooted sound. The author is especially interested in the softer instrumental and psybient side, while the vocal reggae elements may be less aligned with their taste.
17:30
B:R:E:A:K
Ozora Stage
17:30
Sensient
Pumpui
17:30
Sudden Reverb5
The Dome
Minimal electronic music with a restrained, repetitive approach and only limited material currently available. Sudden Reverb may appeal to listeners who enjoy subtle development, although it is not a strong personal priority for the author.

With Andreas Koller.

Petersøn
Melting Pot
19:00
Misled Convoy5
The Dome
Experimental downtempo and dub-influenced electronica with a loose, leftfield character. Misled Convoy may suit listeners who enjoy understated and unconventional sound design, although it did not stand out strongly to the author.
19:30
Noura Mint Seymali3
Dragon Nest
Powerful Mauritanian live music built around vocals, guitar, and driving regional rhythms. Noura Mint Seymali is not a close match for the author's personal festival taste, but could be compelling for listeners seeking intense and expressive global music.

Novelty Engine5
Ozora Stage
Psychedelic rock with a live-band focus rather than a conventional electronic dancefloor structure. Novelty Engine could appeal to listeners who want guitars and a rock-oriented change of pace.

Interactive Exhibition & Live Painting
Mirador
Visionary art exhibition with interactive works by Davide Gomba / Lutopia, Neuromorph, Adél Garics & Péter Fejes, Skywalker Visual, Deepversive Studio, Balázs Fügedi & Manó Tasi, Emma Forgách and Zsanett Kalácska; plus live painters.

Pumpui
21:30
Blacksmith Show
Ironia
22:00
Younger Brother5.5-7.8
Dragon Nest
Psychedelic electronica and progressive rock with emotional songwriting, detailed production, and a strong live-band identity. Younger Brother could appeal to listeners who enjoy the musical world around Shpongle but want a more song-focused experience.
22:00
Altruism8.4
Ozora Stage
Bright, flowing daytime psytrance with uplifting energy and a strong melodic dancefloor feel. Altruism/DJ Thatha is a high recommendation for the author, especially for sharing an energetic daytime set with friends.

Fire Show
Dragon Nest
23:30
Avalon7.4
Ozora Stage
High-energy full-on psytrance with polished production, fast momentum, and relentless dancefloor drive. Avalon can be amazing for the author, although some passages feel too hard or monotonous; in the right festival state, the set could still be powerful.

Thursday 30 July

00:00
Efdemin
Pumpui
00:30
Cinema
Ambyss
00:30
Ott.6.6-8.4
Dragon Nest
Warm psychedelic dub and downtempo with detailed production, organic color, and a playful immersive flow. Ott is a major name and could work beautifully in a psychedelic setting, although the author personally connects even more strongly with Hedflux or Dekel's downtempo material.
01:00
GMS & Dickster6.6-7.9
Ozora Stage
Classic full-on psytrance with experienced dancefloor control and a bright, driving energy. GMS & Dickster could be especially good if the set leans toward their daytime sound.

Pumpui

Benji Vaughan

Phil Hartnoll (Orbital)6.0-8.4
Dragon Nest
Chilled electronic music connected to the musical world of Orbital, with a melodic and spacious character. Phil Hartnoll (Orbital) could appeal to listeners who appreciate classic British electronica and a gentler festival journey.

17:00
Rumpistol6.0-7.5
Ambyss
Tranquil, detailed electronica with a relaxed and slightly experimental character. Rumpistol feels worth trying for listeners who enjoy calm, carefully produced music and a softer Iboga-associated atmosphere.

Bellegance & Fraser6.7-7.7
Melting Pot
Progressive electronic music with a steady, flowing dancefloor character. Bellegance & Fraser could appeal to listeners who enjoy gradual development and a less aggressive festival groove.
19:30
Star Sounds Orchestra
Ozora Stage
20:30
Circus Cabaret
Ambyss
20:30
Circus Cabaret
Cosmic Theater
20:30
Banyek2.5-5.5
The Dome
Unconventional electronic music with bizarre and challenging sound choices. Banyek is unlikely to match most of the author's taste, but could appeal to adventurous listeners who enjoy strange textures and unpredictable movement.
21:00
Light Art & Body Painting
Artibarn Workshops
Lutòpia El & Radio · Neon Tribal Zentangle Slide Painting · Lumaddicts Lumi'naissant Body Paint Facepaint, Uv Bodypaint · Alien Portals

B:R:E:A:K

21:00
Interactive Exhibition & Live Painting
Mirador
Visionary art exhibition with interactive works by Davide Gomba / Lutopia, Neuromorph, Adél Garics & Péter Fejes, Skywalker Visual, Deepversive Studio, Balázs Fügedi & Manó Tasi, Emma Forgách and Zsanett Kalácska; plus live painters.
21:00
Astral Projection7
Ozora Stage
An iconic act rooted in classic Goa and psytrance, with melodic sequences and an unmistakably old-school spirit. Astral Projection is not the author's personal favorite, but remains an important and potentially joyful choice for fans of the genre's foundations.
21:00
Ewake
Pumpui
21:30
Blacksmith Show
Ironia
21:30
Om Unit3.5-7.5
The Dome
UK bass music that can move through techno, dubstep, jungle, and other broken-beat territory. Om Unit is not a strong personal match for the author, but some passages could be interesting and the live setting may reveal more than the available tracks.
22:00
Wild Marmalade6.2-8
Dragon Nest
Live tribal dance music driven by didgeridoo, percussion, and raw physical energy. Wild Marmalade is a highly appealing option for the author and could be ideal for listeners who want to move wildly to an organic groove.
22:30
Sounds from the Melting Pot
Melting Pot
22:30
Neo Shaman5
Ozora Stage
Early-night psytrance with spacey, disorienting textures and a darker direction. Neo Shaman may suit listeners who enjoy less melodic psychedelic territory, but it is probably too abstract for the author's preference.
22:30
Jossie Telch
Pumpui
22:30
Anatolian Weapons3.0-6.0
The Dome
Experimental and unconventional electronic music with unusual rhythms and leftfield structures. Anatolian Weapons could appeal to listeners who enjoy abstract sounds, creative movement, and stepping outside familiar dancefloor patterns, though it is not a close match for the author's taste.
23:00
Dance and Theatre impro jam
Ambyss
23:00
Dance And Theatre Impro Jam
Cosmic Theater
23:00
Stargazing

23:30
Fire Show

Friday 31 July

00:00
Lumaddicts - Live Act Visual Show
Artibarn Workshops
00:00
Tündérke
Ozora Stage
Reliable information about Tünderke could not be confirmed when this guide was prepared. Treat the set as an open discovery slot rather than relying on an uncertain musical description.
00:00
Yuli Fershtat
Pumpui
00:30
Cinema
Ambyss
00:30
My Baby3
Dragon Nest
Rock-oriented live music with prominent female vocals and a band-focused sound. My baby is not what the author personally wants from a psytrance festival, but could appeal to listeners looking for a strong live-rock alternative.
00:30
Jane Fitz4.5-7.6
The Dome
Deep, patient electronic music built around long-form selection, hypnotic grooves, and gradual development. Jane Fitz could appeal to listeners who enjoy immersive, slow-burning sets that move beyond a single narrow genre.
01:30
Rawar
Ozora Stage
01:30
Jedidiah
Pumpui
02:30
Felix Laband5.0-7.5
Dragon Nest
Relaxed downtempo electronica with touches of techno and understated EDM. Felix Laband could provide a pleasant place to come down and breathe, although the author does not consider it essential within a psytrance festival.

Chambok House · Mikayel Zolyan
Led by Mikayel Zolyan.
10:30
Modus8.1
Ozora Stage
Modern progressive psytrance with detailed sound design, strong grooves, and a polished psychedelic flow. Modus is a high-priority choice for the author and could appeal to listeners who want melodic depth without losing dancefloor power.
11:00
Lazy Calm Raga
Ambyss
11:00
Ales and Pictures with Lili Melocco
Bloomy Kids
11:00
Mavvi & Antonia
Cooking Groove
Reliable information about this exact collaboration was not available, and the Antonia material found online may refer to a different artist. The available sound appeared close to pop, so Mavvi and Antonia remain an uncertain discovery slot.

Spell Jar
Herb Hut

Bollywood Dance
Lotus Tent · Jenil Dholakia
With Jenil Dholakia.
12:00
Skizologic6.8-8.4
Ozora Stage
Melodic psytrance with a classic psychedelic character, driving grooves, and detailed progressive movement. Skizologic sounded very promising to the author and feels well worth trying live.
12:00
Mirror System6.3-7.2
The Dome
Ambient and downtempo electronica with soft rhythms, spacious textures, and a reflective late-night mood. Mirror System could appeal to listeners looking for an unhurried, immersive alternative to the main dancefloors.
12:30
Biomigrant7.0-8.0
Cooking Groove
Electronic music with Latin American vocals, live instruments, and a warm cross-cultural character. Biomigrant looks like a worthwhile discovery for listeners who enjoy organic performance blended with modern production.
12:30
Antique
Pumpui

Blue Planet Corporation
Ozora Stage
14:00
Sorian6.3-8.0
Cooking Groove
Melodic tech and chill influences with Arabic color, shifting between relaxed passages and more energetic danceable sections. Sorian could be worth exploring if the timetable is open, although the intensity may become tiring over a long set.

Britta Arnold
Pumpui
14:00
SavaBorsa & RichieTyerra7-9.5
The Dome
Light, distinctive downtempo with forest textures, Spanish-language elements, and Latin American warmth. Savaborsa & RichiTierra are among the author's strongest discoveries for listeners who want a unique, relaxed, and colorful journey.

Melting Pot
15:00
Tiny But Mighty:nature's Micro-Survivors
Microcosmos
15:00
Etnica4
Ozora Stage
Classic Goa and old-school psytrance with a historically important melodic sound. Etnica is not closely aligned with the author's current taste, but could be an excellent match for listeners who love the genre's early style.
15:30
Anicca2.5
Cooking Groove
Live guitar music with a rock-oriented feel, based on the material the author was able to identify. Anicca is not a personal match for this guide, but may appeal to listeners seeking a non-electronic band experience.
15:30
In Between
Pumpui
15:30
Chillum Trio
The Dome
16:30
Anatolian Weapons3.0-6.0
Ambyss
Experimental and unconventional electronic music with unusual rhythms and leftfield structures. Anatolian Weapons could appeal to listeners who enjoy abstract sounds, creative movement, and stepping outside familiar dancefloor patterns, though it is not a close match for the author's taste.
16:30
HeartWave Telepathy Workshop
Bodhi Tent · NéliaHolistica
With NéliaHolistica.

Ginko Stage · Petraflow + dj Kornwall
With Petraflow + dj Kornwall.
17:00
Urban Vinyl
Melting Pot
17:00
Moonclipse7.4
Pumpui
Progressive electronic dance music with a steady, accessible flow built for continuous movement. Moonclipse could appeal to listeners who want a set they can comfortably dance through from beginning to end.
17:00
Zen Baboon7.4-8.5
The Dome
Soft, melodic psychedelic downtempo with an easy flow, warm atmosphere, and organic detail. Zen Baboon is a strong recommendation for listeners who want an emotional, relaxed, and deeply pleasant journey.
17:30
Spoink3.5-5.5
Dragon Nest
A highly varied live-band performance that can move through techno, psychedelic, and deliberately bizarre territory. Spoink may attract listeners who enjoy unpredictable fusion, although the overall style is not a strong match for the author.
18:00
Somatic Slow Flow, Release & Presence
Bodhi Tent · Jade
With Jade.
18:00
Triabal Dream Catcher
Herb Hut
18:00
Creative Dance Flow
Lotus Tent · Daniela Valente
With Daniela Valente.
18:30
Andras Toth
Ambyss
18:30
Enrico Sangiuliano
Pumpui
Driving modern techno with polished production, long builds, and a powerful club-focused flow. Enrico Sangiuliano is a favorite of one of the author's friends and could appeal to listeners looking for a focused techno experience.
19:00
Dance the Beyond
Ginko Stage · Valentina Karmi & Dj Nostress
With Valentina Karmi & Dj Nostress.
19:00
Baladi
Melting Pot
19:00
MΣo Culpa7.0-8.6
The Dome
Melodic, approachable electronic music with an easy flow that strongly appeals to the author. MEO Culpa is a high-priority discovery and also appears in another collaboration with Zakhorov at Cooking Groove.
19:30
ColorStar2.0-5.0
Dragon Nest
20:00
John 00 Fleming5.9-8.6
Pumpui
A wide-ranging blend of techno, trance, and psychedelic electronic music designed for sustained dancing. John 00 Fleming could be a strong choice for open-minded listeners, although the author would prefer to hear the set on a larger stage with more powerful sound.
20:30
Veronika Szabó - Kingdom
Ambyss
20:30
Veronika Szabó - Kingdom
Cosmic Theater
20:30
Shpongle8-8.4
Ozora Stage
A classic and highly distinctive blend of psybient, world music, electronic production, and psychedelic live instrumentation. Shpongle is fascinating and unique, although the author sometimes prefers the studio tracks to live sets that lean more heavily into rock and drums.
21:00
Light Art & Body Painting: Lutòpia El & Radio
Artibarn Workshops
Slide Painting · Tribal & Uv Makeup Experience Lumi'naissant Body Paint Facepaint, Uv Bodypaint

Melting Pot
21:00
Interactive Exhibition & Live Painting
Mirador
Visionary art exhibition with interactive works by Davide Gomba / Lutopia, Neuromorph, Adél Garics & Péter Fejes, Skywalker Visual, Deepversive Studio, Balázs Fügedi & Manó Tasi, Emma Forgách and Zsanett Kalácska; plus live painters.
21:00
Rumpistol6.0-7.5
The Dome
Tranquil, detailed electronica with a relaxed and slightly experimental character. Rumpistol feels worth trying for listeners who enjoy calm, carefully produced music and a softer Iboga-associated atmosphere.
21:30
Blacksmith Show
Ironia
21:30
Tom_ato
Pumpui
22:00
Gaudi + Don Letts + Earl 161.5-5.5
Dragon Nest
Reggae and dub blended with psychedelic electronic touches and a strong vocal presence. Gaudi and Don Letts and Earl 16 is not a close match for the author's taste, but could appeal to listeners who enjoy soundsystem culture and live reggae energy.
22:00
Grant Darshan
Ozora Stage
22:30
Sounds from the Melting Pot
Melting Pot
22:30
Eclektic
The Dome
Eclektic also appears elsewhere on the Dome programme, but reliable stylistic notes were limited when this guide was prepared. Consider this a flexible discovery option rather than a firmly described recommendation.

Fire Show
Dragon Nest
23:30
Eat Static4.0-8.0
Ozora Stage
Psychedelic electronic music with a darker, energetic edge and a wide stylistic range. Eat Static may be especially interesting for listeners who appreciate unusual crossover territory, though its heavier moments do not always match the author's taste.

Saturday 1 August

00:00
Griffin Kloud
The Dome
00:30
Cinema
Ambyss
00:30
Dubsahara meets Vlastur4.2-8.2
Dragon Nest
Dubsahara meets vlastur brings together relaxing psychill and darker psy-dub: Dubsahara begins in a spacious Dome-like mood, while Vlastur adds heavier bass and a more shadowy direction. The collaboration is not essential for the author, but could suit listeners who enjoy deep dub journeys and gradual shifts into stranger territory.
01:00
Giuseppe
Ozora Stage
01:00
Sindh - Live
Pumpui
01:30
Herrhausen & Treindl7.0-8.1
The Dome
Highly progressive, melodic trance with a flowing emotional character. Herrhausen & Treindl are a strong recommendation for listeners who want to settle into a warm, evolving journey.
02:30
Pistamashinano rating
Dragon Nest
The available recording quality was too poor for a confident personal assessment. pistamashina appears to explore a disorienting mix of electronica, glitchy pop, and occasional cinematic flourishes, so this may suit listeners who enjoy hard-to-classify leftfield performance.

Ozora Stage
Modern psytrance with strong connections to established names and a promising, high-impact direction. Asgard is still a relatively new discovery for the author, but feels exciting enough to make this a priority live experience.

Easy-listening electronic music with a warm, accessible flow and a collaborative character. Meo Culpa and Zakhorov sound like a promising option for listeners who want something enjoyable without an overly demanding edge.

Talpa7.9
Ozora Stage
Distinctive full-on psytrance with playful sound design, melodic detail, and a slightly unusual psychedelic personality. Talpa is a high-priority first live experience for the author, whose favorite material includes the remix of Headroom's “Acid Trip Advisor.”

Astrix8.3
Ozora Stage
Polished full-on and progressive psytrance with memorable melodies, powerful builds, and a highly accessible main-stage flow. Astrix is one of the author's strongest recommendations, especially for visitors who are still discovering psytrance.

Atmos7.3
Ozora Stage
Classic progressive psytrance with steady grooves and a clean, hypnotic flow. Atmos is often a strong match for the author's taste, although a previous Boom performance felt less convincing than expected.
14:00
Nora Matis
Pumpui
14:30
Zen Baboon7.4-8.5
Ambyss
Soft, melodic psychedelic downtempo with an easy flow, warm atmosphere, and organic detail. Zen Baboon is a strong recommendation for listeners who want an emotional, relaxed, and deeply pleasant journey.
15:30
Headroom7.9
Ozora Stage
Progressive psytrance with muscular grooves, memorable hooks, and a playful but driving psychedelic edge. Headroom is a high-priority choice for the author, with tracks such as “Acid Trip Advisor,” “EDMdma,” and “Anxiety Society” standing out strongly.
15:30
Andras Bader
Pumpui
15:30
Nautis
The Dome
16:00
O:F:F
Ambyss
17:00
Enki
Dragon Nest
17:00
Grouch6.2-7.5
Ozora Stage
Psychedelic electronica that often connects progressive grooves with bass-heavy, funky, and slower-moving textures. Grouch could appeal to listeners who enjoy detailed production and a journey that sits between dancefloor energy and more spacious psychedelic territory.
17:00
Infragandhi
Pumpui
17:00
Dubapest
The Dome
18:30
O:F:F
Ozora Stage
18:30
Bernathy
Pumpui
18:30
Lucas in dub
The Dome
Reliable information about Lucas In Dub was limited when this guide was prepared. Treat the performance as an open discovery slot rather than a recommendation based on an uncertain artist match.
19:30
Heimya
Dragon Nest
20:00
Atia
Pumpui
20:30
Globular7.5-8.4
The Dome
Warm psychedelic dub and downtempo with playful bass, organic detail, and imaginative remix work. Globular feels like an excellent way to close the festival and is one of the author's stronger recommendations.
21:00
Interactive Exhibition & Live Painting
Mirador
Visionary art exhibition with interactive works by Davide Gomba / Lutopia, Neuromorph, Adél Garics & Péter Fejes, Skywalker Visual, Deepversive Studio, Balázs Fügedi & Manó Tasi, Emma Forgách and Zsanett Kalácska; plus live painters.
